frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, prenups should become normal in India too. Aajkal marriage is also a financial risk for men, and with such one-sided laws and all the 498A misuse stories, I can understand why so many men are scared. Wanting basic legal protection doesn't make them anti-women.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women collectively as a legal/financial threat to men and normalizes the idea that laws protecting women are broadly misused. It encodes men's-rights victimhood inversion and legal framing around 'one-sided laws' and '498A misuse.'
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
